name: extend
summary: control how the centerline is extended. <spec> may be one of the following keywords normal/reverse = extend given and following stations to the same/reverse direction as previous station. If station names are given, direction is applied only to the specified station or shot. left/right = same as above, but direction is specified explicitly. vertical = do not move station (shot) in X direction, use only Z component of the shot start = specify starting station (shot) ignore = ignore specified station (shot), continue extended elevation with other station (shot) if possible hide = do not show specified station (shot) in extended elevation or a percentage in the range 0 to 200. This sets the extend ratio, i.e.\ the factor used for the horizontal component of a leg in extended elevation. Therion keeps the vertical component unchanged and multiplies only the horizontal displacement by the ratio. Thus 100 means the normal width of the extended leg, 50 compresses it to one half, 200 stretches it to double width, and 0 suppresses any horizontal displacement (effectively the same result as vertical for that leg). The optional station arguments are interpreted as follows: extend <spec> = set the default extend behaviour for subsequent shots in the current centreline. extend <spec> <from> = apply the extend setting to a station. extend <spec> <from> <to> = apply the extend setting to a specific shot. extend <spec> <before> <from> <to> = apply the extend setting to a specific shot, with an additional before station condition. This form is primarily useful with ignore, where the shot is ignored only if the extended-elevation traversal reaches <from> from <before>. Station names use the normal Therion object reference syntax, so station@survey may be used.
